rulings.Case Title: CORNELIUS MURPHY et al VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (BP) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Bind Plaintiffs To Their Original Discovery Responses Regarding Witness Identification (C.C.P. § 2030.310 (B));
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Discovery Calendar for Thursday, January 19, 2012 in Department 503 at 9:00 a.m., Line 1. Defendant Kaiser Gypsum Company Inc.¿s motion to bind Plaintiffs to their original discovery responses regarding witness identification is denied. Defendant failed to demonstrate that Plaintiff¿s earlier omission of a witness was willful and that no alternative remedy exists. Plaintiffs identified the witness prior to the discovery cut-off date, and Defendant fails to show why a continuance would not be a sufficient remedy. Defendant¿s and plaintiff¿s requests for sanctions are denied. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 10:15am. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If a court reporter is needed, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: MELVIN DESIN VS. ASBESTOS CORPORATION LIMITED et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Protective Order To Continue The Deposition Of Melvin Desin
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Discovery Calendar for Thursday, January 19, 2012 in Department 503 at 9:00 a.m., Line 2. Defendants Lorillard Tobacco Company and Hollingsworth and Vose Company¿s motion for a protective order to continue the deposition of Melvin Desin: hearing required. The hearing will be 10:15am. Both sides must bring proposed orders to the hearing. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If a court reporter is needed, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. = (503/TLJ)
